canto_title: The Encounter with Farinata and the Shadows of Dis

scenes.summary: Dante asks Virgil if the souls in the tombs can be seen.
A soul from a tomb explains the nature of the resurrection and the belief of the Epicureans.
Virgil rebukes Dante for his indiscreet way of speaking.
A sudden voice directs Dante to look at the upright figure of Farinata.
Dante observes Farinata’s majestic and defiant posture in the face of Hell.
Farinata questions Dante about his political origins and lineage.
Dante counters Farinata by suggesting his people failed to master the art of war.
A shadow rises from a grave and asks about his missing son.
Dante answers the shadow by indirectly referencing Guido da Montefeltro.
The shadow reacts with shock to the news and collapses in despair.
An unmoved Farinata asks why the living are so wicked toward his people.
Dante responds by comparing the destruction of his people to a blood-stained river.
Farinata mourns his role as the sole defender of Florence during its downfall.
Dante asks for clarification regarding his destiny and the vision of the future.
Farinata explains that souls in Hell can only see things that are far away.
Dante apologizes to Virgil for his previous silence and error.
Virgil identifies other famous figures, such as Frederick II, resting nearby.
Virgil questions why Dante appears so lost and disoriented.
Virgil instructs Dante to remember this moment and look toward Beatrice for guidance.
The poets leave the wall and descend into a foul-smelling valley.
